channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Upper gastrointestinal cancers surpass lung cancer as leading cause of cancer deaths in Australia
item.description: For the first time, upper gastrointestinal (GI) cancers have overtaken lung cancer to become the leading cause of cancer-related deaths in Australia, according to a recent analysis by the Pancare Foun...

item.title: SADC Finance Officials Review Current Budget and Discuss Plans for 2026/27
item.description: On March 9, 2026, senior finance officials from Southern Africa met in Pretoria for a key session of the SADC Finance Committee. The meeting was attended by representatives from 11 of the 16 member st...

item.title: Ukraine Provides Expertise to Countries on Countering Iranian Drones
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that eleven countries have sought Ukraine’s assistance in defending against Iranian-designed drones, which are now being launched by Russia and used i...

item.title: Ireland to invest in vegetable farming and horticulture in Arusha, Tanzania
item.description: Tanzania dey ready to welcome more Irish investment, especially for horticulture and vegetable farming, to boost economic growth and create jobs. Ireland’s Ambassador to Tanzania, Nicola Brennan, meet...

item.title: Nigerian Government Paid Over N2 Billion to Former Niger Delta Militants in 2025
item.description: For the 2025 fiscal year, the Nigerian government reportedly transferred over N2 billion directly into the private accounts of four former Niger Delta militant leaders. According to public payments da...

item.title: Army Jawan Missing During River-Crossing Training in Rajouri, J&K
item.description: On Monday, an Indian Army soldier, Sepoy Akshit Sharma, went missing during a river-crossing training exercise in Sunderbani area, Rajouri district, Jammu and Kashmir. The incident occurred around 12 ...

item.title: Data shows ethnic dominance in staff at select Kenyan public universities
item.description: A recent Nation Africa article exposes ethnic imbalances in Kenya's public universities, highlighting how some institutions have staff dominated by single tribes. The report draws from government data...

item.title: Israeli Airstrikes Target Hezbollah Infrastructure in Beirut
item.description: On March 10, 2026, airstrikes carried out by Israel struck multiple areas in Beirut, Lebanon. The Israeli government confirmed that the attacks were specifically aimed at what it described as Hezbolla...

item.title: Niger State Chief Judge to Sponsor Education of Young Inmate
item.description: The Chief Judge of Niger State, Justice Halima Ibrahim Abdulmalik, don take decision to sponsor education of Gabriel Sillas, a teenage inmate for Minna Correctional Centre, all the way to university l...

item.title: Iranians Reflect on War and Its Impact on Prospects for Political Change
item.description: In the wake of ongoing U.S.-Israeli military attacks on Iran, many Iranians who opposed the Islamic regime express mixed emotions. Initially, some saw these strikes as a potential path to ending nearl...

item.title: Bill Maher discusses Trump dinner with Sam Harris on podcast
item.description: On an episode of his podcast 'Club Random' released on 9 March, comedian and host Bill Maher engaged in a heated discussion with author Sam Harris regarding Maher's attendance at a White House dinner ...

item.title: President Ruto Signs Judges' Retirement Benefits Law
item.description: On Monday, 9th March 2026, President William Ruto signed into law the Judges' Retirement Benefits Bill at State House, Nairobi, marking an important step in strengthening the welfare and independence ...

item.title: Health Minister urges legal action to allow liver transplant for teenager despite religious objections
item.description: Health Minister Aaron Motsoaledi has urged Charlotte Maxeke Johannesburg Academic Hospital to approach the high court to save the life of a 15-year-old boy in need of a liver transplant. The boy, from...

item.title: Peter Obi Condemns Attack on Rotimi Amaechi, Warns of Rising Political Violence in Nigeria
item.description: Peter Obi, the former Labour Party presidential candidate for 2023 and ex-governor of Anambra State, don talk say Nigeria dey enter dangerous era of political thuggery under the current administration...

item.title: Labour MPs pressure government to extend ban on step-family pornography
item.description: The UK government is facing renewed pressure over online pornography regulations, particularly regarding depictions of sexual activity between adult step-relatives. Baroness Gabby Bertin’s recent amen...

item.title: Tips and Examples for Complimenting Your Girlfriend's Instagram Photos
item.description: This article guides Kenyan Instagram users on how to comment thoughtfully on their girlfriend’s pictures to show admiration and love. It explains that simple, sincere compliments on her beauty, smile,...

item.title: Seven Innovative Designs That Command Attention Through Creativity
item.description: Moss and Fog highlights seven exceptional design projects that exemplify how thoughtful design can captivate attention without demanding it. Among them is a credenza by Giorgio Bonaguro featuring a ph...

item.title: Trump discusses military actions, media ratings, and political decisions
item.description: In a highly satirical and sensational piece, former US President Donald Trump is depicted discussing his involvement in foreign conflicts, military strategy, and media coverage. The article describes ...

item.title: Digital Enclosures: Big Tech’s Control and the Need for AI Governance
item.description: Between the 18th and 19th centuries, England implemented the Enclosure Acts, which converted common lands into private property, stripping communities of their rights while granting landowners control...

item.title: Comparing Trump and Bush: Continuities in US Right-Wing Approaches to War
item.description: The essay from The New York Times explores the similarities between Donald Trump’s approach to a potential conflict with Iran and George W. Bush’s invasion of Iraq. Drawing on a 2023 National Review p...
